talk_metadata.talk_title: Representation learning over cancer genomes, scaling petabytes to predictions
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "It's important to recognize that identifying the cause of something is different from predicting an outcome."
Speaker said: "We think that the HR Difuse model itself ... does perform better than both these models by a fair margin."
Speaker said: "We are a bit shocked by how well we're able to do here... approaching 0.75 in terms of F1."

key_data_gaps: No prospective clinical validation of HRDfuse predictions
Limited assessment of non‑BRCA associated cancers

crispro_opportunity.description: HRDfuse improves HRD detection in non‑BRCA associated cancers where existing tools (HRDetect, CORG) underperform.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "When we look at non‑BRCA associated cancers ... we see a big performance difference here" and "HRDfuse does perform better than both these models by a fair margin".
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can leverage the multi‑view neural representation to further refine HRD prediction across diverse tumor types, addressing the gap in current signature‑based tools.

cited_competitors.name: CORG
cited_competitors.context: Random forest‑based HRD classifier used for comparison

unresolved_questions: Why does HRDfuse performance drop in certain non‑BRCA cancers?
Can the model be extended to predict other therapy responses beyond platinum?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Dimensional structural chromosome instability in human cancers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We think HSRs are actually really interesting because they're good news..."

Speaker said: "We call this method JAVEL ... state‑of‑the‑art approach that takes into account rearrangements and copy number as input and outputs what I think is a state‑of‑the‑art copy number profiles."

Speaker said: "We estimate about 30 dimensions of structural SIN across thousands of cancers, and some of these may provide insight into targetable synthetic lethalities detectable only with whole‑genome sequencing."

key_data_gaps: Limited validation of HSR vs ecDNA distinction in patient samples
Unclear clinical impact of many newly defined structural signatures

crispro_opportunity.description: Structural SIN signatures (e.g., BFB, Typhonis) correlate with synthetic lethal vulnerabilities, offering combination therapy targets.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We found a large number of correlations between the burden of these structural SIN signatures and specific... vulnerabilities to individual therapies."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate structural graph features with functional genomics data to prioritize drug combinations for tumors harboring specific SIN signatures.

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate HSR status as a stratification biomarker for HER2‑targeted trials, since HSR‑mediated HER2 amplifications may respond differently than ecDNA‑mediated amplifications.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "If a cancer cell amplifies HER2 on an ecDNA, it's more likely to develop resistance... but if we can detect the subset of HER2 amplifications that are chromosomally integrated, those tumors would have a harder time evading therapy."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can provide robust HSR detection pipelines to enable biomarker‑driven enrollment.

cited_competitors.name: Amplicon Architect
cited_competitors.context: Tool for ecDNA detection compared against the author's HSR pipeline

cited_competitors.name: gGenome
cited_competitors.context: Graph‑genome analysis suite used for structural event detection

unresolved_questions: Can long‑read or Hi‑C technologies definitively resolve HSR vs ecDNA origins?
What is the therapeutic relevance of Typhonis‑derived HSRs in acral melanoma?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Mass General Hospital and Broad Institute
talk_metadata.talk_title: Systematic analysis of >8,000 TCGA whole genomes
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "There is a new sequencing artifact ... we call that a vapor trail and we developed a tool to filter it."
Speaker said: "We find many more mutations in GC‑rich regions that were missed by the older PEACOG data."
Speaker said: "We identified novel non‑coding drivers and structural variation drivers, some of which are completely new."

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: 30‑50X coverage missed mutations in GC‑rich promoters and first exons

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted loss of mutations in GC‑rich regions due to PCR amplification, indicating a technical limitation of the approach.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on low‑coverage, PCR‑based WGS limited driver discovery and biomarker detection.

key_data_gaps: No functional validation of many novel non‑coding and SV drivers
Limited prospective clinical outcome linkage for newly identified drivers

crispro_opportunity.description: Re‑process PEACOG samples with PCR‑free 80X WGS to recover missed driver mutations for previously failed biomarker programs.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We have deeper coverage, PCR‑free ... we are fixing the problem of lack of coverage in important genes."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can re‑analyze existing raw data with its pipeline to rescue missed variants and re‑prioritize targets.

crispro_opportunity.description: Use newly identified non‑coding promoter mutations (e.g., TERT, albumin) as stratification biomarkers for targeted trials.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "The top mutated base in TCGA is one of the TERT promoter mutations..."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s regulatory‑region annotation can rapidly flag promoter hotspots for trial enrollment criteria.

cited_competitors.name: Genomics England
cited_competitors.context: Reference for mutational signature work cited in background

unresolved_questions: What proportion of the novel SV drivers are true oncogenic events versus artifacts?
How do the newly discovered non‑coding drivers impact patient survival and therapy response?
